notes: Layer 2 — behavioral validation against the running app. Layer 1 (automated) is already green: 164 backend tests pass, both trees type-check, check:conventions --strict has 0 backend hard violations. These items need the worktree app running (backend on the project DB + Vite SPA) and a human or computer-use run to fill status/evidence.

items.title: Create a project and add bits (Projects manager)
steps: 1. Open the Projects screen from the nav.
2. Create a project (name + description).
3. Open it; add a few bits across kinds, including a `constraint` bit 'web only' and an `inventory` bit listing the real screens.
items.expected: Project is created and listed; bits appear grouped by kind on the project detail.

items.title: Grounded interview anchors options to real app facts
steps: 1. Start a new interview and attach it to that project via the picker.
2. Generate the first batch for a request like 'redesign the dashboard'.
items.expected: Options reference real app facts (no invented screens like a 'Puzzle page'); grounded options show the 'verify with engineering' note.

items.title: Settled-kind bit suppresses a question, visibly, with override
items.precondition: Project has a settled bit (e.g. constraint 'web only'); interview attached to it; request is a styling/revamp (not a platform change).
steps: 1. Generate a batch where a platform question would normally appear.
2. Observe the 'Skipped — based on your project context' notice.
3. Click 'Answer anyway' on the skipped item and submit a free-text answer.
items.expected: The platform question is not asked; it appears in the skipped notice with a reason; 'Answer anyway' records an override decision visible in the resulting ticket.

items.title: Suppression is conditional on the request
steps: 1. Start an interview whose request is 'expand the app to mobile'.
2. Generate a batch.
items.expected: The platform question IS asked (the request is about changing that fact), i.e. the settled bit does not suppress it.

items.title: Cached grounding prefix (R4)
items.precondition: A grounded session with a project that has enough bits to exceed the cache minimum.
steps: 1. Generate batch 1, then batch 2 for the same grounded session.
2. Inspect the model usage for batch 2 (server logs / response usage).
items.expected: usage.cache_read_input_tokens > 0 on batch 2 (the rules+bits system prefix is read from cache).
items.notes: Requires surfacing usage; may need a temporary log.

items.title: Generate-bits prompt popup
steps: 1. On the project detail, click 'Generate prompt'.
2. Click Copy.
items.expected: A prompt is shown that embeds the bit JSON schema and instructs a separate Claude Code session to recon the repo at the product level and write a JSON file; Copy puts it on the clipboard.

items.title: Import → reconcile → resolve a conflict (additive)
items.precondition: A project with some existing bits.
steps: 1. Click 'Import bits'; paste a JSON file of bits, some overlapping/contradicting existing ones; leave force OFF.
2. Review the reconciliation plan (conflicts/similars surfaced first).
3. Resolve a conflict (merge / keep both); apply.
items.expected: A plan is shown; nothing is written until you resolve+apply; existing bits are NOT cleared; resolved bits land; superseded bits remain for audit.

items.title: Force import replaces existing bits
steps: 1. Import with the 'Replace existing (force)' checkbox ON.
items.expected: Existing active bits are superseded and the imported set becomes the active bits (the only path that clears).

items.title: Ticket grounded in project bits
items.precondition: A grounded session reaches ticket generation.
steps: 1. Complete an interview on a project-attached session and generate the ticket.
items.expected: The ticket's context_summary / acceptance criteria reflect real app facts from the bits (no invented requirements beyond the decisions).

items.title: Merge-on-complete proposes bits from a finalized ticket (T13)
items.precondition: A finalized ticket on a project-attached session.
steps: 1. On the finalized ticket, click 'Merge to project context'.
2. Review the proposed candidate bits + reconciliation plan; resolve; apply.
items.expected: Candidate bits are proposed from the ticket, reconciled against the project, and on apply land with source 'merged' + the source ticket id.

items.title: Code scout is fully removed
steps: 1. Request a former scout route, e.g. POST /sessions/1/scout and POST /internal/scout/process.
2. Boot the server with no GITHUB_TOKEN / SCOUT_WORKER_SECRET / RUN_SCOUT_WORKER set.
items.expected: Former scout routes 404; the server boots cleanly with no scout env; scout_cache/scout_jobs tables no longer exist.
items.notes: Code-level already verified: 24 scout files deleted, app/index/config unwired, drop_scout migration applied (batch 8).

items.title: Ungrounded interview still works (no project attached)
steps: 1. Start an interview WITHOUT selecting a project.
2. Generate a batch and proceed to a ticket.
items.expected: The interview runs on the unchanged ungrounded path (no grounding, nothing suppressed) and produces a ticket — grounding is purely additive.
